# TheURLShortner

> TheURLShortner (https://theurlshortner.com) is a free URL shortener that turns long web
> addresses into short, shareable, trackable links. You can optionally set a custom
> back-half code, protect a link with a password, add an expiration, generate a QR code,
> and track how many times each link is clicked. No signup is required to shorten a URL.

## Key facts
- TheURLShortner is a free URL shortener with click tracking and no signup required.
- Shortening URLs, custom codes, click tracking, QR codes, password protection and expirations are all free.
- No account is needed to shorten a link; a free account adds a dashboard to create, edit and delete links.
- Custom short codes (the "back-half") can be 4–30 letters, numbers or dashes.
- Every short link automatically counts its clicks.
- Links can optionally be password-protected; the destination stays hidden until the correct password is entered.
- Links can optionally expire after 1 hour, 24 hours, 7 days or 30 days; by default they never expire.
- Every short link comes with a free, downloadable SVG QR code.
- A free public REST API is available (no API key, CORS enabled) to shorten links and read stats.
- Built with Node.js, Express and a libSQL/Turso database; hosted on Vercel.

## Pricing
- Free. There are no paid plans; every feature listed above is available at no cost.

## Common questions
- What is a URL shortener? A tool that converts a long URL into a short link that redirects to the original address.
- How do I shorten a URL on TheURLShortner? Paste the long URL on the homepage and click "Shorten"; optionally add a custom code, password or expiration.
- Is TheURLShortner free? Yes. Shortening URLs is free and needs no account.
- Can I customize the short link? Yes, set a custom back-half code (4–30 letters, numbers or dashes).
- Can I track clicks? Yes, each link shows its total click count.
- Can I password-protect a link? Yes, set a password when shortening; visitors must enter it before being forwarded.
- Do short links expire? Not by default; you can optionally set a 1h, 24h, 7d or 30d expiration.
- Is there an API? Yes, a free REST API with no key. See the docs.

## Pages
- Home: https://theurlshortner.com/
- API docs: https://theurlshortner.com/docs
- Guides index: https://theurlshortner.com/guides
- What is a URL shortener?: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/what-is-a-url-shortener
- How to shorten a URL: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/how-to-shorten-a-url
- Custom short links: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/how-to-create-a-custom-short-link
- Free URL shortener (no signup): https://theurlshortner.com/guides/free-url-shortener
- URL shortener with click tracking: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/url-shortener-with-click-tracking
- QR code for a link: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/qr-code-for-a-link
- Shorten a YouTube link: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/shorten-youtube-link
- Instagram bio link shortener: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/instagram-bio-link-shortener
- Shorten a link for WhatsApp: https://theurlshortner.com/guides/shorten-link-for-whatsapp
- Sign in / Sign up: https://theurlshortner.com/login
